title: Rathbunia beneckei
intro: Rathbunia beneckei, also known as the Mexican fleabane, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can help to alleviate symptoms of various conditions. Rathbunia beneckei contains bioactive constituents including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Rathbunia beneckei is often used to prepare infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for skin issues and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Rathbunia beneckei is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is commonly known as the desert marigold. Rathbunia beneckei is a daisy-like perennial that produces bright yellow flowers in the early spring. Its classification also includes the subfamily Barnadesioideae.
